CREATE TEMP TABLE tmp_empty_views AS
SELECT view_name, view_type, row_count,
    CASE 
        WHEN view_name LIKE $1 THEN $2
        WHEN view_name LIKE $3 THEN $4
        WHEN view_name LIKE $5 THEN $6
        WHEN view_name LIKE $7 THEN $8
        WHEN view_name LIKE $9 THEN $10
        WHEN view_name LIKE $11 THEN $12
        ELSE $13
    END AS view_category
FROM (
    -- For materialized views, use pg_class.reltuples (fast)
    SELECT c.relname AS view_name, $14 AS view_type, c.reltuples::bigint AS row_count
    FROM pg_class c
    JOIN pg_namespace n ON n.oid = c.relnamespace
    WHERE n.nspname = $15 AND c.relkind = $16
    -- Note: Regular views require actual count - skip them in empty check
    -- They were already counted in Phase 1
) v
WHERE row_count = $17
ORDER BY view_category, view_name;

SELECT current_database() AS "TABLE_CAT", n.nspname AS "TABLE_SCHEM", c.relname AS "TABLE_NAME",  CASE n.nspname ~ $3 OR n.nspname = $4  WHEN $5 THEN CASE  WHEN n.nspname = $6 OR n.nspname = $7 THEN CASE c.relkind   WHEN $8 THEN $9   WHEN $10 THEN $11   WHEN $12 THEN $13   ELSE $14   END  WHEN n.nspname = $15 THEN CASE c.relkind   WHEN $16 THEN $17   WHEN $18 THEN $19   ELSE $20   END  ELSE CASE c.relkind   WHEN $21 THEN $22   WHEN $23 THEN $24   WHEN $25 THEN $26   WHEN $27 THEN $28   WHEN $29 THEN $30   ELSE $31   END  END  WHEN $32 THEN CASE c.relkind  WHEN $33 THEN $34  WHEN $35 THEN $36  WHEN $37 THEN $38  WHEN $39 then $40  WHEN $41 THEN $42  WHEN $43 THEN $44  WHEN $45 THEN $46  WHEN $47 THEN $48  WHEN $49 THEN $50  ELSE $51  END  ELSE $52  END  AS "TABLE_TYPE", d.description AS "REMARKS",  $53 as "TYPE_CAT", $54 as "TYPE_SCHEM", $55 as "TYPE_NAME", $56 AS "SELF_REFERENCING_COL_NAME", $57 AS "REF_GENERATION"  FROM pg_catalog.pg_namespace n, pg_catalog.pg_class c  LEFT JOIN pg_catalog.pg_description d ON (c.oid = d.objoid AND d.objsubid = $58  and d.classoid = $59::regclass)  WHERE c.relnamespace = n.oid  AND n.nspname LIKE $1 AND c.relname LIKE $2 AND ($60  OR ( c.relkind = $61 AND n.nspname <> $62 AND n.nspname <> $63 ) )  ORDER BY "TABLE_TYPE","TABLE_SCHEM","TABLE_NAME"

CREATE TEMP TABLE tmp_extraction_stats AS
WITH view_temporal_classification AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN matviewname IS NOT NULL THEN matviewname
            ELSE viewname
        END AS view_name,
        c.temporal_granularity
    FROM (
        SELECT viewname, $1::text AS matviewname FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $2
        UNION ALL
        SELECT $3::text AS viewname, matviewname FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $4
    ) v
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL cia_classify_temporal_view(COALESCE(matviewname, viewname)) AS c
),
temporal_summary AS (
    SELECT 
        temporal_granularity,
        COUNT(*) AS view_count,
        CASE temporal_granularity
            WHEN $5 THEN $6
            WHEN $7 THEN $8
            WHEN $9 THEN $10
            WHEN $11 THEN $12
            WHEN $13 THEN $14
            ELSE $15
        END AS sampling_strategy
    FROM view_temporal_classification
    GROUP BY temporal_granularity
),
overall_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        $16 AS category,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_tables WHERE schemaname = $17 AND tablename NOT LIKE $18 AND tablename NOT LIKE $19) AS total_tables,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $20) AS total_views,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $21) AS total_materialized_views,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $22) + (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $23) AS total_views_all,
        $24::int AS default_sample_size,
        $25::int AS party_sample_size,
        $26::int AS committee_sample_size,
        $27::int AS person_sample_size,
        $28::int AS document_sample_size,
        $29::int AS voting_sample_size,
        $30::int AS worldbank_sample_size,
        $31::int AS complete_threshold
)
SELECT 
    $32 AS metric,
    temporal_granularity AS granularity,
    view_count::bigint AS count,
    sampling_strategy AS strategy
FROM temporal_summary
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $33 AS metric,
    $34 AS granularity,
    (total_tables + total_views_all)::bigint AS count,
    $35 || total_tables || $36 || total_views || $37 || total_materialized_views AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $38 AS metric,
    $39 AS granularity,
    default_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $40 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $41 AS metric,
    $42 AS granularity,
    party_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $43 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $44 AS metric,
    $45 AS granularity,
    document_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $46 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $47 AS metric,
    $48 AS granularity,
    voting_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $49 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $50 AS metric,
    $51 AS granularity,
    worldbank_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $52 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $53 AS metric,
    $54 AS granularity,
    complete_threshold::bigint AS count,
    $55 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
ORDER BY metric, granularity;

WITH categorical_columns AS (
    SELECT 
        c.table_name,
        c.column_name,
        c.data_type,
        c.character_maximum_length
    FROM information_schema.columns c
    JOIN information_schema.tables t 
        ON t.table_schema = c.table_schema 
        AND t.table_name = c.table_name
    WHERE c.table_schema = $1
      AND t.table_type = $2
      AND c.data_type IN ($3 /*, ... */)
      -- Exclude large text fields and known non-categorical columns
      AND (c.character_maximum_length IS NULL OR c.character_maximum_length <= $4)
      -- ========================================================================
      -- EXCLUDE CONTENT/TEXT FIELDS (long text, descriptions, etc.)
      -- ========================================================================
      AND c.column_name NOT IN ($5 /*, ... */)
      -- ========================================================================
      -- EXCLUDE URL FIELDS
      -- ========================================================================
      AND c.column_name NOT IN ($6 /*, ... */)
      -- ========================================================================
      -- EXCLUDE NAME FIELDS (person names, full names, etc.)
      -- ========================================================================
      AND c.column_name NOT IN ($7 /*, ... */)
      -- ========================================================================
      -- EXCLUDE ID FIELDS (unique identifiers, foreign keys, etc.)
      -- ========================================================================
      AND c.column_name NOT LIKE $8
      AND c.column_name NOT LIKE $9
      AND c.column_name NOT LIKE $10
      AND c.column_name != $11
      AND c.column_name NOT LIKE $12
      AND c.column_name NOT LIKE $13
      AND c.column_name NOT LIKE $14
      AND c.column_name NOT LIKE $15
      AND c.column_name NOT LIKE $16
      AND c.column_name NOT LIKE $17
      AND c.column_name NOT LIKE $18
      -- ========================================================================
      -- EXCLUDE SESSION/USER TRACKING FIELDS
      -- ========================================================================
      AND c.column_name NOT IN ($19 /*, ... */)
      -- ========================================================================
      -- EXCLUDE DATE/TIME STRING FIELDS
      -- ========================================================================
      AND c.column_name NOT LIKE $20
      AND c.column_name NOT LIKE $21
      AND c.column_name NOT IN ($22 /*, ... */)
      -- ========================================================================
      -- EXCLUDE TECHNICAL/INFRASTRUCTURE TABLES (not domain data)
      -- ========================================================================
      -- Javers audit tables (jv_*)
      AND c.table_name NOT LIKE $23
      -- Quartz scheduler tables (qrtz_*)
      AND c.table_name NOT LIKE $24
      -- Liquibase migration tables
      AND c.table_name NOT IN ($25 /*, ... */)
      -- Hibernate sequence table
      AND c.table_name != $26
      -- Encrypted value storage (sensitive)
      AND c.table_name != $27
      -- Application session/event tables (user tracking, not political data)
      AND c.table_name NOT IN ($28 /*, ... */)
      -- User account tables (sensitive)
      AND c.table_name NOT IN ($29 /*, ... */)
      -- Portal configuration tables
      AND c.table_name NOT IN ($30 /*, ... */)
      -- Rule violation tracking
      AND c.table_name != $31
      -- Data source metadata
      AND c.table_name != $32
      -- Language/translation tables (not political data)
      AND c.table_name NOT IN ($33 /*, ... */)
      -- ========================================================================
      -- EXCLUDE OTHER HIGH-CARDINALITY OR NON-CATEGORICAL COLUMNS
      -- ========================================================================
      AND c.column_name NOT IN ($34 /*, ... */)
      -- ========================================================================
      -- Skip columns already extracted above (critical columns)
      -- ========================================================================
      AND NOT (c.table_name = $35 AND c.column_name IN ($36 /*, ... */))
      AND NOT (c.table_name = $37 AND c.column_name IN ($38 /*, ... */))
      AND NOT (c.table_name = $39 AND c.column_name IN ($40 /*, ... */))
      AND NOT (c.table_name = $41 AND c.column_name IN ($42 /*, ... */))
      AND NOT (c.table_name = $43 AND c.column_name IN ($44 /*, ... */))
      AND NOT (c.table_name = $45 AND c.column_name IN ($46 /*, ... */))
      AND NOT (c.table_name = $47 AND c.column_name IN ($48))
      AND NOT (c.table_name = $49 AND c.column_name IN ($50 /*, ... */))
      AND NOT (c.table_name = $51 AND c.column_name IN ($52 /*, ... */))
    ORDER BY c.table_name, c.column_name
)

WITH view_counts AS (
    SELECT schemaname,
           viewname,
           row_count
    FROM cia_view_row_counts
    WHERE viewname != $1
      AND viewname != $2
      AND viewname NOT LIKE $3
      -- Skip views that timed out or had errors during Phase 1
      -- row_count: -1 = timeout, -2 = error, 0+ = success
      AND row_count >= $4
),
-- ============================================================================
-- TEMPORAL VIEW CLASSIFICATION
-- ============================================================================
-- Identify views by temporal granularity based on naming patterns
-- This enables stratified sampling across time periods
-- Uses reusable cia_classify_temporal_view() function
-- ============================================================================
view_temporal_classification AS (
    SELECT 
        vc.schemaname,
        vc.viewname,
        vc.row_count,
        c.temporal_granularity,
        c.samples_per_bucket
    FROM view_counts vc
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL cia_classify_temporal_view(vc.viewname) AS c
    -- CHANGED: Include ALL views, even empty ones (for header-only CSV files)
    -- WHERE vc.row_count > 0  -- REMOVED: No longer skip empty views
),
-- ============================================================================
-- TEMPORAL COLUMN DETECTION
-- ============================================================================
-- Automatically detect temporal columns in each view
-- ============================================================================
view_temporal_columns AS (
    SELECT 
        c.table_schema AS schemaname,
        c.table_name AS viewname,
        c.column_name AS temporal_column,
        c.data_type,
        -- Prioritize common temporal column names
        CASE 
            WHEN c.column_name IN ($5 /*, ... */) THEN $6
            WHEN c.column_name IN ($7 /*, ... */) THEN $8
            WHEN c.column_name IN ($9 /*, ... */) THEN $10
            WHEN c.column_name LIKE $11 THEN $12
            ELSE $13
        END AS priority
    FROM information_schema.columns c
    WHERE c.table_schema = $14
      AND c.data_type IN ($15 /*, ... */)
      AND c.column_name NOT IN ($16 /*, ... */) -- Exclude technical columns
),
view_primary_temporal_column AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT ON (schemaname, viewname)
        schemaname,
        viewname,
        temporal_column
    FROM view_temporal_columns
    ORDER BY schemaname, viewname, priority
),
-- ============================================================================
-- GENERATE STRATIFIED SAMPLING QUERIES
-- ============================================================================
view_extract AS (
    SELECT 
        vtc.schemaname,
        vtc.viewname,
        vtc.row_count,
        vtc.temporal_granularity,
        vtc.samples_per_bucket,
        vptc.temporal_column,
        -- Smart sample size calculation:
        -- 1. Complete extraction for small views (< threshold)
        -- 2. Extended samples for analytical trend views (election proximity, seasonal activity, career trajectory)
        -- 3. Extended samples for party/committee/person views
        -- 4. Extended samples for document/voting/worldbank views
        -- 5. Default sample size for others
        CASE 
            -- Complete extraction for small views
            WHEN vtc.row_count <= $17::int THEN vtc.row_count
            -- Extended samples for analytical trend views (500 rows for better temporal coverage)
            WHEN vtc.viewname IN ($18 /*, ... */) THEN
                LEAST($19::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for party-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $20 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $21 THEN 
                LEAST($22::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for committee-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $23 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $24 THEN 
                LEAST($25::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for person/politician views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $26 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $27 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $28 THEN 
                LEAST($29::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for document-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $30 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $31 THEN 
                LEAST($32::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for voting-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $33 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $34 THEN 
                LEAST($35::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for worldbank data
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $36 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $37 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $38 THEN 
                LEAST($39::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Default sample size
            ELSE LEAST($40::int, vtc.row_count)
        END AS sample_rows,
        -- Extraction type for logging
        CASE 
            WHEN vtc.row_count <= $41::int THEN $42
            WHEN vtc.viewname IN ($43 /*, ... */) THEN $44
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $45 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $46 THEN $47
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $48 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $49 THEN $50
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $51 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $52 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $53 THEN $54
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $55 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $56 THEN $57
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $58 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $59 THEN $60
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $61 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $62 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $63 THEN $64
            ELSE $65
        END AS extraction_type,
        CASE WHEN vtc.viewname LIKE $66 THEN vtc.viewname ELSE $67 || vtc.viewname END AS file_prefix
    FROM view_temporal_classification vtc
    LEFT JOIN view_primary_temporal_column vptc 
        ON vtc.schemaname = vptc.schemaname 
        AND vtc.viewname = vptc.viewname
)
-- ============================================================================
-- GENERATE EXTRACTION COMMANDS WITH TEMPORAL STRATIFICATION
-- ============================================================================
SELECT 
    CASE 
        -- =====================================================================
        -- DAILY VIEWS: Stratified sampling by day
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $68 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $69 || $70 ||
                $71 ||
                $72 ||
                $73 ||
                $74 ||
                $75 ||
                $76 ||
                $77 ||
                $78 ||
                $79 || $80 ||
                $81 || $82 || $83 || $84 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $85 THEN $86 || $87
                    ELSE $88 || $89
                END,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname, temporal_column,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- WEEKLY VIEWS: Stratified sampling by week
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $90 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $91 || $92 ||
                $93 ||
                $94 ||
                $95 ||
                $96 ||
                $97 ||
                $98 ||
                $99 ||
                $100 ||
                $101 || $102 ||
                $103 || $104 || $105 || $106 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $107 THEN $108 || $109
                    ELSE $110 || $111
                END,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname, temporal_column,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- MONTHLY VIEWS: Stratified sampling by month
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $112 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $113 || $114 ||
                $115 ||
                $116 ||
                $117 ||
                $118 ||
                $119 ||
                $120 ||
                $121 ||
                $122 ||
                $123 || $124 ||
                $125 || $126 || $127 || $128 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $129 THEN $130 || $131
                    ELSE $132 || $133
                END,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname, temporal_column,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- ANNUAL VIEWS: Stratified sampling by year
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $134 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $135 || $136 ||
                $137 ||
                $138 ||
                $139 ||
                $140 ||
                $141 ||
                $142 ||
                $143 ||
                $144 || $145 ||
                $146 || $147 || $148 || $149 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $150 THEN $151 || $152
                    ELSE $153 || $154
                END,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- TEMPORAL TREND VIEWS: Sample across all time periods
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $155 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $156 || $157 ||
                $158 ||
                $159 ||
                $160 ||
                $161 ||
                $162 ||
                $163 ||
                $164 ||
                $165 ||
                $166 || $167 ||
                $168 || $169 || $170 || $171 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $172 THEN $173 || $174
                    ELSE $175 || $176
                END,
                viewname,
                temporal_column, temporal_column, schemaname, viewname,
                sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- NON-TEMPORAL VIEWS: Use random sampling with smart sample sizes
        -- =====================================================================
        ELSE
            format(
                $177 || $178 ||
                $179 || $180 ||
                $181 || $182 || $183 || $184 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $185 THEN $186 || $187
                    ELSE $188 || $189
                END,
                extraction_type,
                viewname,
                sample_rows,
                row_count,
                schemaname,
                viewname,
                sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix,
                file_prefix
            )
    END AS extraction_command
FROM view_extract
ORDER BY 
    CASE temporal_granularity
        WHEN $190 THEN $191
        WHEN $192 THEN $193
        WHEN $194 THEN $195
        WHEN $196 THEN $197
        WHEN $198 THEN $199
        ELSE $200
    END,
    viewname

WITH RECURSIVE 
        -- Get all materialized view dependencies
        view_deps AS (
            SELECT DISTINCT
                dependent_view.relname AS view_name,
                source_table.relname AS depends_on,
                CASE 
                    WHEN source_table.relkind = $1 THEN $2
                    ELSE $3
                END AS depends_on_matview
            FROM pg_depend
            JOIN pg_rewrite ON pg_depend.objid = pg_rewrite.oid
            JOIN pg_class AS dependent_view ON pg_rewrite.ev_class = dependent_view.oid
            JOIN pg_class AS source_table ON pg_depend.refobjid = source_table.oid
            JOIN pg_namespace dependent_ns ON dependent_ns.oid = dependent_view.relnamespace
            JOIN pg_namespace source_ns ON source_ns.oid = source_table.relnamespace
            WHERE dependent_view.relkind = $4
              AND source_table.relkind IN ($5 /*, ... */)
              AND pg_depend.deptype = $6
              AND dependent_ns.nspname = $7
              AND source_ns.nspname = $8
        ),
        -- Build dependency graph (only MV dependencies)
        mv_dependencies AS (
            SELECT view_name, depends_on
            FROM view_deps
            WHERE depends_on_matview = $9
        ),
        -- Get all materialized views
        all_mvs AS (
            SELECT matviewname AS view_name
            FROM pg_matviews
            WHERE schemaname = $10
        ),
        -- Calculate dependency depth using recursive CTE
        dependency_depth AS (
            -- Base case: views with no MV dependencies
            SELECT 
                am.view_name,
                $11 AS depth,
                ARRAY[am.view_name] AS path
            FROM all_mvs am
            WHERE NOT EXISTS (
                SELECT $12 FROM mv_dependencies md 
                WHERE md.view_name = am.view_name
            )
            
            UNION ALL
            
            -- Recursive case: views that depend on views we've already processed
            SELECT 
                md.view_name,
                dd.depth + $13,
                dd.path || md.view_name
            FROM mv_dependencies md
            JOIN dependency_depth dd ON md.depends_on = dd.view_name
            WHERE NOT (md.view_name = ANY(dd.path)) -- Prevent cycles
        ),
        -- Get maximum depth for each view
        max_depths AS (
            SELECT 
                view_name,
                MAX(depth) as max_depth
            FROM dependency_depth
            GROUP BY view_name
        )
        -- Output in refresh order
        SELECT 
            $14 AS schemaname,
            view_name AS matviewname,
            max_depth AS dependency_level
        FROM max_depths
        ORDER BY max_depth, view_name

SELECT current_database() AS "TABLE_CAT", n.nspname AS "TABLE_SCHEM", c.relname AS "TABLE_NAME",  CASE n.nspname ~ $3 OR n.nspname = $4  WHEN $5 THEN CASE  WHEN n.nspname = $6 OR n.nspname = $7 THEN CASE c.relkind   WHEN $8 THEN $9   WHEN $10 THEN $11   WHEN $12 THEN $13   ELSE $14   END  WHEN n.nspname = $15 THEN CASE c.relkind   WHEN $16 THEN $17   WHEN $18 THEN $19   ELSE $20   END  ELSE CASE c.relkind   WHEN $21 THEN $22   WHEN $23 THEN $24   WHEN $25 THEN $26   WHEN $27 THEN $28   WHEN $29 THEN $30   ELSE $31   END  END  WHEN $32 THEN CASE c.relkind  WHEN $33 THEN $34  WHEN $35 THEN $36  WHEN $37 THEN $38  WHEN $39 then $40  WHEN $41 THEN $42  WHEN $43 THEN $44  WHEN $45 THEN $46  WHEN $47 THEN $48  WHEN $49 THEN $50  ELSE $51  END  ELSE $52  END  AS "TABLE_TYPE", d.description AS "REMARKS",  $53 as "TYPE_CAT", $54 as "TYPE_SCHEM", $55 as "TYPE_NAME", $56 AS "SELF_REFERENCING_COL_NAME", $57 AS "REF_GENERATION"  FROM pg_catalog.pg_namespace n, pg_catalog.pg_class c  LEFT JOIN pg_catalog.pg_description d ON (c.oid = d.objoid AND d.objsubid = $58  and d.classoid = $59::regclass)  WHERE c.relnamespace = n.oid  AND n.nspname LIKE $1 AND c.relname LIKE $2 AND ($60  OR ( c.relkind = $61 AND n.nspname !~ $62 AND n.nspname <> $63 )  OR ( c.relkind = $64 AND n.nspname !~ $65 AND n.nspname <> $66 ) )  ORDER BY "TABLE_TYPE","TABLE_SCHEM","TABLE_NAME"

CREATE TEMP TABLE tmp_extraction_stats AS
WITH view_temporal_classification AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN matviewname IS NOT NULL THEN matviewname
            ELSE viewname
        END AS view_name,
        c.temporal_granularity
    FROM (
        SELECT viewname, $1::text AS matviewname FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $2
        UNION ALL
        SELECT $3::text AS viewname, matviewname FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $4
    ) v
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL cia_classify_temporal_view(COALESCE(matviewname, viewname)) AS c
),
temporal_summary AS (
    SELECT 
        temporal_granularity,
        COUNT(*) AS view_count,
        CASE temporal_granularity
            WHEN $5 THEN $6
            WHEN $7 THEN $8
            WHEN $9 THEN $10
            WHEN $11 THEN $12
            WHEN $13 THEN $14
            ELSE $15
        END AS sampling_strategy
    FROM view_temporal_classification
    GROUP BY temporal_granularity
),
overall_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        $16 AS category,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_tables WHERE schemaname = $17 AND tablename NOT LIKE $18 AND tablename NOT LIKE $19) AS total_tables,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $20) AS total_views,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $21) AS total_materialized_views,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $22) + (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $23) AS total_views_all,
        $24::int AS default_sample_size,
        $25::int AS party_sample_size,
        $26::int AS committee_sample_size,
        $27::int AS person_sample_size,
        $28::int AS document_sample_size,
        $29::int AS voting_sample_size,
        $30::int AS worldbank_sample_size,
        $31::int AS complete_threshold
)
SELECT 
    $32 AS metric,
    temporal_granularity AS granularity,
    view_count::bigint AS count,
    sampling_strategy AS strategy
FROM temporal_summary
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $33 AS metric,
    $34 AS granularity,
    (total_tables + total_views_all)::bigint AS count,
    $35 || total_tables || $36 || total_views || $37 || total_materialized_views AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $38 AS metric,
    $39 AS granularity,
    default_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $40 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $41 AS metric,
    $42 AS granularity,
    party_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $43 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $44 AS metric,
    $45 AS granularity,
    document_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $46 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $47 AS metric,
    $48 AS granularity,
    voting_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $49 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $50 AS metric,
    $51 AS granularity,
    worldbank_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $52 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $53 AS metric,
    $54 AS granularity,
    complete_threshold::bigint AS count,
    $55 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
ORDER BY metric, granularity

WITH view_counts AS (
    SELECT schemaname,
           viewname,
           row_count
    FROM cia_view_row_counts
    WHERE viewname != $1
      AND viewname != $2
      AND viewname NOT LIKE $3
      -- Skip views that timed out or had errors during Phase 1
      -- row_count: -1 = timeout, -2 = error, 0+ = success
      AND row_count >= $4
),
-- ============================================================================
-- TEMPORAL VIEW CLASSIFICATION
-- ============================================================================
-- Identify views by temporal granularity based on naming patterns
-- This enables stratified sampling across time periods
-- Uses reusable cia_classify_temporal_view() function
-- ============================================================================
view_temporal_classification AS (
    SELECT 
        vc.schemaname,
        vc.viewname,
        vc.row_count,
        c.temporal_granularity,
        c.samples_per_bucket
    FROM view_counts vc
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL cia_classify_temporal_view(vc.viewname) AS c
    -- CHANGED: Include ALL views, even empty ones (for header-only CSV files)
    -- WHERE vc.row_count > 0  -- REMOVED: No longer skip empty views
),
-- ============================================================================
-- TEMPORAL COLUMN DETECTION
-- ============================================================================
-- Automatically detect temporal columns in each view
-- ============================================================================
view_temporal_columns AS (
    SELECT 
        c.table_schema AS schemaname,
        c.table_name AS viewname,
        c.column_name AS temporal_column,
        c.data_type,
        -- Prioritize common temporal column names
        CASE 
            WHEN c.column_name IN ($5 /*, ... */) THEN $6
            WHEN c.column_name IN ($7 /*, ... */) THEN $8
            WHEN c.column_name IN ($9 /*, ... */) THEN $10
            WHEN c.column_name LIKE $11 THEN $12
            ELSE $13
        END AS priority
    FROM information_schema.columns c
    WHERE c.table_schema = $14
      AND c.data_type IN ($15 /*, ... */)
      AND c.column_name NOT IN ($16 /*, ... */) -- Exclude technical columns
),
view_primary_temporal_column AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT ON (schemaname, viewname)
        schemaname,
        viewname,
        temporal_column
    FROM view_temporal_columns
    ORDER BY schemaname, viewname, priority
),
-- ============================================================================
-- GENERATE STRATIFIED SAMPLING QUERIES
-- ============================================================================
view_extract AS (
    SELECT 
        vtc.schemaname,
        vtc.viewname,
        vtc.row_count,
        vtc.temporal_granularity,
        vtc.samples_per_bucket,
        vptc.temporal_column,
        -- Smart sample size calculation:
        -- 1. Complete extraction for small views (< threshold)
        -- 2. Extended samples for analytical trend views (election proximity, seasonal activity, career trajectory)
        -- 3. Extended samples for party/committee/person views
        -- 4. Extended samples for document/voting/worldbank views
        -- 5. Default sample size for others
        CASE 
            -- Complete extraction for small views
            WHEN vtc.row_count <= $17::int THEN vtc.row_count
            -- Extended samples for analytical trend views (500 rows for better temporal coverage)
            WHEN vtc.viewname IN ($18 /*, ... */) THEN
                LEAST($19::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for party-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $20 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $21 THEN 
                LEAST($22::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for committee-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $23 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $24 THEN 
                LEAST($25::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for person/politician views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $26 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $27 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $28 THEN 
                LEAST($29::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for document-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $30 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $31 THEN 
                LEAST($32::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for voting-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $33 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $34 THEN 
                LEAST($35::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for worldbank data
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $36 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $37 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $38 THEN 
                LEAST($39::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Default sample size
            ELSE LEAST($40::int, vtc.row_count)
        END AS sample_rows,
        -- Extraction type for logging
        CASE 
            WHEN vtc.row_count <= $41::int THEN $42
            WHEN vtc.viewname IN ($43 /*, ... */) THEN $44
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $45 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $46 THEN $47
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $48 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $49 THEN $50
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $51 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $52 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $53 THEN $54
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $55 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $56 THEN $57
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $58 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $59 THEN $60
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $61 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $62 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $63 THEN $64
            ELSE $65
        END AS extraction_type,
        CASE WHEN vtc.viewname LIKE $66 THEN vtc.viewname ELSE $67 || vtc.viewname END AS file_prefix
    FROM view_temporal_classification vtc
    LEFT JOIN view_primary_temporal_column vptc 
        ON vtc.schemaname = vptc.schemaname 
        AND vtc.viewname = vptc.viewname
)
-- ============================================================================
-- GENERATE EXTRACTION COMMANDS WITH TEMPORAL STRATIFICATION
-- ============================================================================
SELECT 
    CASE 
        -- =====================================================================
        -- DAILY VIEWS: Stratified sampling by day
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $68 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $69 || $70 ||
                $71 ||
                $72 ||
                $73 ||
                $74 ||
                $75 ||
                $76 ||
                $77 ||
                $78 ||
                $79 || $80 ||
                $81 || $82 || $83 || $84 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $85 THEN $86 || $87
                    ELSE $88 || $89
                END,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname, temporal_column,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- WEEKLY VIEWS: Stratified sampling by week
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $90 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $91 || $92 ||
                $93 ||
                $94 ||
                $95 ||
                $96 ||
                $97 ||
                $98 ||
                $99 ||
                $100 ||
                $101 || $102 ||
                $103 || $104 || $105 || $106 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $107 THEN $108 || $109
                    ELSE $110 || $111
                END,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname, temporal_column,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- MONTHLY VIEWS: Stratified sampling by month
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $112 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $113 || $114 ||
                $115 ||
                $116 ||
                $117 ||
                $118 ||
                $119 ||
                $120 ||
                $121 ||
                $122 ||
                $123 || $124 ||
                $125 || $126 || $127 || $128 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $129 THEN $130 || $131
                    ELSE $132 || $133
                END,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname, temporal_column,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- ANNUAL VIEWS: Stratified sampling by year
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $134 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $135 || $136 ||
                $137 ||
                $138 ||
                $139 ||
                $140 ||
                $141 ||
                $142 ||
                $143 ||
                $144 || $145 ||
                $146 || $147 || $148 || $149 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $150 THEN $151 || $152
                    ELSE $153 || $154
                END,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- TEMPORAL TREND VIEWS: Sample across all time periods
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $155 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $156 || $157 ||
                $158 ||
                $159 ||
                $160 ||
                $161 ||
                $162 ||
                $163 ||
                $164 ||
                $165 ||
                $166 || $167 ||
                $168 || $169 || $170 || $171 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $172 THEN $173 || $174
                    ELSE $175 || $176
                END,
                viewname,
                temporal_column, temporal_column, schemaname, viewname,
                sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- NON-TEMPORAL VIEWS: Use random sampling with smart sample sizes
        -- =====================================================================
        ELSE
            format(
                $177 || $178 ||
                $179 || $180 ||
                $181 || $182 || $183 || $184 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $185 THEN $186 || $187
                    ELSE $188 || $189
                END,
                extraction_type,
                viewname,
                sample_rows,
                row_count,
                schemaname,
                viewname,
                sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix,
                file_prefix
            )
    END AS extraction_command
FROM view_extract
ORDER BY 
    CASE temporal_granularity
        WHEN $190 THEN $191
        WHEN $192 THEN $193
        WHEN $194 THEN $195
        WHEN $196 THEN $197
        WHEN $198 THEN $199
        ELSE $200
    END,
    viewname

CREATE TEMP TABLE tmp_extraction_stats AS
WITH view_temporal_classification AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN matviewname IS NOT NULL THEN matviewname
            ELSE viewname
        END AS view_name,
        c.temporal_granularity
    FROM (
        SELECT viewname, $1::text AS matviewname FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $2
        UNION ALL
        SELECT $3::text AS viewname, matviewname FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $4
    ) v
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL cia_classify_temporal_view(COALESCE(matviewname, viewname)) AS c
),
temporal_summary AS (
    SELECT 
        temporal_granularity,
        COUNT(*) AS view_count,
        CASE temporal_granularity
            WHEN $5 THEN $6
            WHEN $7 THEN $8
            WHEN $9 THEN $10
            WHEN $11 THEN $12
            WHEN $13 THEN $14
            ELSE $15
        END AS sampling_strategy
    FROM view_temporal_classification
    GROUP BY temporal_granularity
),
overall_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        $16 AS category,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_tables WHERE schemaname = $17 AND tablename NOT LIKE $18 AND tablename NOT LIKE $19) AS total_tables,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $20) AS total_views,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $21) AS total_materialized_views,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $22) + (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $23) AS total_views_all,
        $24::int AS default_sample_size,
        $25::int AS party_sample_size,
        $26::int AS committee_sample_size,
        $27::int AS person_sample_size,
        $28::int AS document_sample_size,
        $29::int AS voting_sample_size,
        $30::int AS worldbank_sample_size,
        $31::int AS complete_threshold
)
SELECT 
    $32 AS metric,
    temporal_granularity AS granularity,
    view_count::bigint AS count,
    sampling_strategy AS strategy
FROM temporal_summary
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $33 AS metric,
    $34 AS granularity,
    (total_tables + total_views_all)::bigint AS count,
    $35 || total_tables || $36 || total_views || $37 || total_materialized_views AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $38 AS metric,
    $39 AS granularity,
    default_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $40 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $41 AS metric,
    $42 AS granularity,
    party_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $43 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $44 AS metric,
    $45 AS granularity,
    document_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $46 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $47 AS metric,
    $48 AS granularity,
    voting_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $49 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $50 AS metric,
    $51 AS granularity,
    worldbank_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $52 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $53 AS metric,
    $54 AS granularity,
    complete_threshold::bigint AS count,
    $55 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
ORDER BY metric, granularity

SELECT current_database() AS "PKTABLE_CAT", pkn.nspname AS "PKTABLE_SCHEM", pkc.relname AS "PKTABLE_NAME", pka.attname AS "PKCOLUMN_NAME", current_database() AS "FKTABLE_CAT", fkn.nspname AS "FKTABLE_SCHEM", fkc.relname AS "FKTABLE_NAME", fka.attname AS "FKCOLUMN_NAME", pos.n AS "KEY_SEQ", CASE con.confupdtype  WHEN $3 THEN $4 WHEN $5 THEN $6 WHEN $7 THEN $8 WHEN $9 THEN $10 WHEN $11 THEN $12 WHEN $13 THEN $14 ELSE $15 END AS "UPDATE_RULE", CASE con.confdeltype  WHEN $16 THEN $17 WHEN $18 THEN $19 WHEN $20 THEN $21 WHEN $22 THEN $23 WHEN $24 THEN $25 WHEN $26 THEN $27 ELSE $28 END AS "DELETE_RULE", con.conname AS "FK_NAME", pkic.relname AS "PK_NAME", CASE  WHEN con.condeferrable AND con.condeferred THEN $29 WHEN con.condeferrable THEN $30 ELSE $31 END AS "DEFERRABILITY"  FROM  pg_catalog.pg_namespace pkn, pg_catalog.pg_class pkc, pg_catalog.pg_attribute pka,  pg_catalog.pg_namespace fkn, pg_catalog.pg_class fkc, pg_catalog.pg_attribute fka,  pg_catalog.pg_constraint con,  pg_catalog.generate_series($32, $33) pos(n),  pg_catalog.pg_class pkic WHERE pkn.oid = pkc.relnamespace AND pkc.oid = pka.attrelid AND pka.attnum = con.confkey[pos.n] AND con.confrelid = pkc.oid  AND fkn.oid = fkc.relnamespace AND fkc.oid = fka.attrelid AND fka.attnum = con.conkey[pos.n] AND con.conrelid = fkc.oid  AND con.contype = $34 AND (pkic.relkind = $35 OR pkic.relkind = $36) AND pkic.oid = con.conindid  AND fkn.nspname = $1 AND fkc.relname = $2 ORDER BY pkn.nspname,pkc.relname, con.conname,pos.n

SELECT
a.attrelid,
a.attnum,
a.attname,
a.attstattarget,
a.attstorage,
t.typstorage,
a.atthasdef,
a.attisdropped,
a.attlen,
a.attalign,
a.attislocal,
pg_catalog.format_type(t.oid, a.atttypmod) AS atttypname,
array_to_string(a.attoptions, $1) AS attoptions,
CASE WHEN a.attcollation <> t.typcollation THEN a.attcollation ELSE $2 END AS attcollation,
pg_catalog.array_to_string(ARRAY(SELECT pg_catalog.quote_ident(option_name) || $3 || pg_catalog.quote_literal(option_value) FROM pg_catalog.pg_options_to_table(attfdwoptions) ORDER BY option_name), $4) AS attfdwoptions,
co.conname AS notnull_name,
CASE WHEN co.convalidated THEN pt.description ELSE $5 END AS notnull_comment,
CASE WHEN NOT co.convalidated THEN co.oid ELSE $6 END AS notnull_invalidoid,
co.connoinherit AS notnull_noinherit,
co.conislocal AS notnull_islocal,
a.attcompression AS attcompression,
a.attidentity,
CASE WHEN a.atthasmissing AND NOT a.attisdropped THEN a.attmissingval ELSE $7 END AS attmissingval,
a.attgenerated
FROM unnest($8::pg_catalog.oid[]) AS src(tbloid)
JOIN pg_catalog.pg_attribute a ON (src.tbloid = a.attrelid) LEFT JOIN pg_catalog.pg_type t ON (a.atttypid = t.oid)
 LEFT JOIN pg_catalog.pg_constraint co ON (a.attrelid = co.conrelid
   AND co.contype = $9 AND co.conkey = array[a.attnum])
 LEFT JOIN pg_catalog.pg_description pt ON (pt.classoid = co.tableoid AND pt.objoid = co.oid)
WHERE a.attnum > $10::pg_catalog.int2
ORDER BY a.attrelid, a.attnum

FROM pg_class c
LEFT JOIN pg_depend d ON (c.relkind = $15 AND d.classid = $16::regclass AND d.objid = c.oid AND d.objsubid = $17 AND d.refclassid = $18::regclass AND d.deptype IN ($19 /*, ... */))
LEFT JOIN pg_tablespace tsp ON (tsp.oid = c.reltablespace)

SELECT 
    temporal_granularity AS "Granularity",
    COUNT(*) AS "View Count",
    CASE temporal_granularity
        WHEN $1 THEN $2
        WHEN $3 THEN $4
        WHEN $5 THEN $6
        WHEN $7 THEN $8
        WHEN $9 THEN $10
        ELSE $11
    END AS "Sampling Strategy"
FROM (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN matviewname IS NOT NULL THEN matviewname
            ELSE viewname
        END AS view_name,
        c.temporal_granularity
    FROM (
        SELECT viewname, $12::text AS matviewname FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $13
        UNION ALL
        SELECT $14::text AS viewname, matviewname FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $15
    ) v
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL cia_classify_temporal_view(COALESCE(matviewname, viewname)) AS c
) view_classification
GROUP BY temporal_granularity
ORDER BY 
    CASE temporal_granularity
        WHEN $16 THEN $17
        WHEN $18 THEN $19
        WHEN $20 THEN $21
        WHEN $22 THEN $23
        WHEN $24 THEN $25
        ELSE $26
    END

WITH RECURSIVE preference AS (
    SELECT $1 AS max_depth  -- The deeper the recursion goes, the slower it performs.
         , $2 AS min_oid -- user objects only
         , $3::text AS schema_exclusion
         , $4::text AS class_exclusion
         , $5::json AS type_ranks),
               dependency_pair AS (
                   WITH relation_object AS ( SELECT oid, oid::regclass::text AS object_name  FROM pg_class )
                   SELECT DISTINCT                          substring(pg_identify_object(classid, objid, $6)::text, $7) as referenced_schema_name,                          CASE classid
                              WHEN $8::regclass THEN (SELECT CONTYPE::text FROM pg_constraint WHERE oid = objid)
                              ELSE objid::text
                              END AS CONTYPE,
                         CASE classid
                              WHEN $9::regclass THEN (SELECT attname FROM pg_attrdef d JOIN pg_attribute c ON (c.attrelid,c.attnum)=(d.adrelid,d.adnum) WHERE d.oid = objid)
                              WHEN $10::regclass THEN (SELECT concat(castsource::regtype::text, $11, casttarget::regtype::text,$12, castfunc::regprocedure::text) FROM pg_cast WHERE oid = objid)
                              WHEN $13::regclass THEN rel.object_name
                              WHEN $14::regclass THEN (SELECT conname FROM pg_constraint WHERE oid = objid)
                              WHEN $15::regclass THEN (SELECT extname FROM pg_extension WHERE oid = objid)
                              WHEN $16::regclass THEN (SELECT nspname FROM pg_namespace WHERE oid = objid)
                              WHEN $17::regclass THEN (SELECT opcname FROM pg_opclass WHERE oid = objid)
                              WHEN $18::regclass THEN (SELECT oprname FROM pg_operator WHERE oid = objid)
                              WHEN $19::regclass THEN (SELECT opfname FROM pg_opfamily WHERE oid = objid)
                              WHEN $20::regclass THEN objid::regprocedure::text
                              WHEN $21::regclass THEN (SELECT ev_class::regclass::text FROM pg_rewrite WHERE oid = objid)
                              WHEN $22::regclass THEN (SELECT tgname FROM pg_trigger WHERE oid = objid)
                              WHEN $23::regclass THEN objid::regtype::text
                              ELSE objid::text
                              END AS REFERENCED_NAME,
                          substring(pg_identify_object(refclassid, refobjid, $24)::text, $25) as referencing_schema_name,                           CASE refclassid
                              WHEN $26::regclass THEN (SELECT nspname FROM pg_namespace WHERE oid = refobjid)
                              WHEN $27::regclass THEN rrel.object_name
                              WHEN $28::regclass THEN (SELECT opfname FROM pg_opfamily WHERE oid = refobjid)
                              WHEN $29::regclass THEN refobjid::regprocedure::text
                              WHEN $30::regclass THEN refobjid::regtype::text
                              ELSE refobjid::text
                              END AS REFERENCING_NAME
                   FROM pg_depend dep
                            LEFT JOIN relation_object rel ON rel.oid = dep.objid
                            LEFT JOIN relation_object rrel ON rrel.oid = dep.refobjid, preference
                   WHERE deptype = ANY($31)
                     AND objid >= preference.min_oid
                     AND (refobjid >= preference.min_oid OR refobjid = $32) -- need public schema as root node
                     AND classid::regclass::text !~ preference.class_exclusion
                     AND refclassid::regclass::text !~ preference.class_exclusion
                     AND COALESCE(SUBSTRING(objid::regclass::text, $33),$34) !~ preference.schema_exclusion
                     AND COALESCE(SUBSTRING(refobjid::regclass::text, $35),$36) !~ preference.schema_exclusion
                   GROUP BY classid, objid, refclassid, refobjid, deptype, rel.object_name, rrel.object_name
               )
 select referenced_schema_name,
    (CASE
      WHEN position($37 in referenced_name) >$38 THEN substring(referenced_name from position($39 in referenced_name)+$40 for length(referenced_name))
      ELSE referenced_name
    END)  AS referenced_name, 
   referencing_schema_name,
   (CASE
      WHEN position($41 in referencing_name) >$42 THEN substring(referencing_name from position($43 in referencing_name)+$44 for length(referencing_name))
      ELSE referencing_name
    END)  AS referencing_name from dependency_pair where REFERENCED_NAME != REFERENCING_NAME  AND ( REFERENCED_NAME like $45 OR REFERENCED_NAME NOT LIKE $46)
 AND (CONTYPE::text != $47 AND CONTYPE::text != $48)
 AND referencing_schema_name is not null and referencing_name is not null

SELECT * FROM (SELECT current_database() AS current_database, n.nspname,c.relname,a.attname,a.atttypid,a.attnotnull  OR (t.typtype = $1 AND t.typnotnull) AS attnotnull,a.atttypmod,a.attlen,t.typtypmod,row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY a.attrelid ORDER BY a.attnum) AS attnum, nullif(a.attidentity, $2) as attidentity,nullif(a.attgenerated, $3) as attgenerated,pg_catalog.pg_get_expr(def.adbin, def.adrelid) AS adsrc,dsc.description,t.typbasetype,t.typtype  FROM pg_catalog.pg_namespace n  JOIN pg_catalog.pg_class c ON (c.relnamespace = n.oid)  JOIN pg_catalog.pg_attribute a ON (a.attrelid=c.oid)  JOIN pg_catalog.pg_type t ON (a.atttypid = t.oid)  LEFT JOIN pg_catalog.pg_attrdef def ON (a.attrelid=def.adrelid AND a.attnum = def.adnum)  LEFT JOIN pg_catalog.pg_description dsc ON (c.oid=dsc.objoid AND a.attnum = dsc.objsubid)  LEFT JOIN pg_catalog.pg_class dc ON (dc.oid=dsc.classoid AND dc.relname=$4)  LEFT JOIN pg_catalog.pg_namespace dn ON (dc.relnamespace=dn.oid AND dn.nspname=$5)  WHERE c.relkind in ($6 /*, ... */) and a.attnum > $7 AND NOT a.attisdropped  AND n.nspname LIKE $8 AND c.relname LIKE $9) c WHERE $10  AND attname LIKE $11 ORDER BY nspname,c.relname,attnum

SELECT 
    temporal_granularity AS "Granularity",
    COUNT(*) AS "View Count",
    CASE temporal_granularity
        WHEN $1 THEN $2
        WHEN $3 THEN $4
        WHEN $5 THEN $6
        WHEN $7 THEN $8
        WHEN $9 THEN $10
        ELSE $11
    END AS "Sampling Strategy"
FROM (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN matviewname IS NOT NULL THEN matviewname
            ELSE viewname
        END AS view_name,
        c.temporal_granularity
    FROM (
        SELECT viewname, $12::text AS matviewname FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $13
        UNION ALL
        SELECT $14::text AS viewname, matviewname FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $15
    ) v
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL cia_classify_temporal_view(COALESCE(matviewname, viewname)) AS c
) view_classification
GROUP BY temporal_granularity
ORDER BY 
    CASE temporal_granularity
        WHEN $16 THEN $17
        WHEN $18 THEN $19
        WHEN $20 THEN $21
        WHEN $22 THEN $23
        WHEN $24 THEN $25
        ELSE $26
    END

SELECT     tmp.TABLE_CAT AS "TABLE_CAT",     tmp.TABLE_SCHEM AS "TABLE_SCHEM",     tmp.TABLE_NAME AS "TABLE_NAME",     tmp.NON_UNIQUE AS "NON_UNIQUE",     tmp.INDEX_QUALIFIER AS "INDEX_QUALIFIER",     tmp.INDEX_NAME AS "INDEX_NAME",     tmp.TYPE AS "TYPE",     tmp.ORDINAL_POSITION AS "ORDINAL_POSITION",     trim(both $2 from pg_catalog.pg_get_indexdef(tmp.CI_OID, tmp.ORDINAL_POSITION, $3)) AS "COLUMN_NAME",   CASE tmp.AM_NAME     WHEN $4 THEN CASE tmp.I_INDOPTION[tmp.ORDINAL_POSITION - $5] & $6::smallint       WHEN $7 THEN $8       ELSE $9     END     ELSE $10   END AS "ASC_OR_DESC",     tmp.CARDINALITY AS "CARDINALITY",     tmp.PAGES AS "PAGES",     tmp.FILTER_CONDITION AS "FILTER_CONDITION",     tmp.REMARKS AS "REMARKS"FROM (SELECT current_database() AS TABLE_CAT,  n.nspname AS TABLE_SCHEM,   ct.relname AS TABLE_NAME, NOT i.indisunique AS NON_UNIQUE,   $11 AS INDEX_QUALIFIER, ci.relname AS INDEX_NAME,   CASE i.indisclustered     WHEN $12 THEN $13    ELSE CASE am.amname       WHEN $14 THEN $15      ELSE $16    END   END AS TYPE,   (information_schema._pg_expandarray(i.indkey)).n AS ORDINAL_POSITION,   ci.reltuples AS CARDINALITY,   ci.relpages AS PAGES,   pg_catalog.pg_get_expr(i.indpred, i.indrelid) AS FILTER_CONDITION,   ci.oid AS CI_OID,   i.indoption AS I_INDOPTION,   am.amname AS AM_NAME,   d.description AS REMARKS FROM pg_catalog.pg_class ct   JOIN pg_catalog.pg_namespace n ON (ct.relnamespace = n.oid)   JOIN pg_catalog.pg_index i ON (ct.oid = i.indrelid)   JOIN pg_catalog.pg_class ci ON (ci.oid = i.indexrelid)   JOIN pg_catalog.pg_am am ON (ci.relam = am.oid)   LEFT JOIN pg_catalog.pg_description d ON (ci.oid = d.objoid) WHERE $17  AND ct.relname = $1) AS tmp ORDER BY "NON_UNIQUE", "TYPE", "INDEX_NAME", "ORDINAL_POSITION"

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION cia_percentile_sample(
    p_table_name text,
    p_column_name text,
    p_order_by text DEFAULT NULL
)
RETURNS TABLE (
    percentile_label text,
    percentile_value numeric,
    row_data jsonb
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $$
DECLARE
    v_sql text;
    v_order_by text;
BEGIN
    -- Default to column name if no order_by specified
    v_order_by := COALESCE(p_order_by, p_column_name);
    
    -- Build dynamic SQL to extract percentile samples using PERCENT_RANK()
    -- Design decision: PERCENT_RANK() directly calculates a continuous percentile
    -- position for each row, allowing precise matching of specific percentiles
    -- (P1, P10, P25, etc.) without relying on fixed NTILE(100) buckets
    v_sql := format('
        WITH ranked_data AS (
            SELECT 
                %I AS column_value,
                row_to_json(t.*)::jsonb AS row_data,
                PERCENT_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY %I) AS pct_rank
            FROM %I AS t
            WHERE %I IS NOT NULL
        ),
        target_percentiles AS (
            SELECT unnest(ARRAY[0.01, 0.10, 0.25, 0.50, 0.75, 0.90, 0.99]) AS target_pct,
                   unnest(ARRAY[''P1'', ''P10'', ''P25'', ''P50'', ''P75'', ''P90'', ''P99'']) AS percentile_label
        ),
        closest_rows AS (
            SELECT DISTINCT ON (tp.percentile_label)
                tp.percentile_label,
                rd.column_value AS percentile_value,
                rd.row_data
            FROM target_percentiles tp
            CROSS JOIN LATERAL (
                SELECT column_value, row_data, pct_rank
                FROM ranked_data
                WHERE pct_rank >= tp.target_pct
                ORDER BY pct_rank, %I
                LIMIT 1
            ) rd
        )
        SELECT percentile_label, percentile_value, row_data
        FROM closest_rows
        ORDER BY percentile_value
    ', p_column_name, p_column_name, p_table_name, p_column_name, v_order_by);
    
    RETURN QUERY EXECUTE v_sql;
EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
    RAISE NOTICE 'ERROR in cia_percentile_sample for %.%: %', p_table_name, p_column_name, SQLERRM;
    RETURN;
END;
$$

CREATE TEMP TABLE tmp_extraction_stats AS
WITH view_temporal_classification AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN matviewname IS NOT NULL THEN matviewname
            ELSE viewname
        END AS view_name,
        c.temporal_granularity
    FROM (
        SELECT viewname, $1::text AS matviewname FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $2
        UNION ALL
        SELECT $3::text AS viewname, matviewname FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $4
    ) v
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL cia_classify_temporal_view(COALESCE(matviewname, viewname)) AS c
),
temporal_summary AS (
    SELECT 
        temporal_granularity,
        COUNT(*) AS view_count,
        CASE temporal_granularity
            WHEN $5 THEN $6
            WHEN $7 THEN $8
            WHEN $9 THEN $10
            WHEN $11 THEN $12
            WHEN $13 THEN $14
            ELSE $15
        END AS sampling_strategy
    FROM view_temporal_classification
    GROUP BY temporal_granularity
),
overall_stats AS (
    SELECT 
        $16 AS category,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_tables WHERE schemaname = $17 AND tablename NOT LIKE $18 AND tablename NOT LIKE $19) AS total_tables,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $20) AS total_views,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $21) AS total_materialized_views,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $22) + (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $23) AS total_views_all,
        $24::int AS default_sample_size,
        $25::int AS party_sample_size,
        $26::int AS committee_sample_size,
        $27::int AS person_sample_size,
        $28::int AS document_sample_size,
        $29::int AS voting_sample_size,
        $30::int AS worldbank_sample_size,
        $31::int AS complete_threshold
)
SELECT 
    $32 AS metric,
    temporal_granularity AS granularity,
    view_count::bigint AS count,
    sampling_strategy AS strategy
FROM temporal_summary
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $33 AS metric,
    $34 AS granularity,
    (total_tables + total_views_all)::bigint AS count,
    $35 || total_tables || $36 || total_views || $37 || total_materialized_views AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $38 AS metric,
    $39 AS granularity,
    default_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $40 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $41 AS metric,
    $42 AS granularity,
    party_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $43 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $44 AS metric,
    $45 AS granularity,
    document_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $46 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $47 AS metric,
    $48 AS granularity,
    voting_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $49 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $50 AS metric,
    $51 AS granularity,
    worldbank_sample_size::bigint AS count,
    $52 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    $53 AS metric,
    $54 AS granularity,
    complete_threshold::bigint AS count,
    $55 AS strategy
FROM overall_stats
ORDER BY metric, granularity;

SELECT 
    temporal_granularity AS "Granularity",

        WHEN $7 THEN $8
        WHEN $9 THEN $10

    END AS "Sampling Strategy"
FROM (

            WHEN matviewname IS NOT NULL THEN matviewname
            ELSE viewname
        END AS view_name,
        c.temporal_granularity

        UNION ALL
        SELECT $14::text AS viewname, matviewname FROM pg_matviews WHERE schemaname = $15

GROUP BY temporal_granularity
ORDER BY

        WHEN $16 THEN $17
        WHEN $18 THEN $19

        WHEN $22 THEN $23

CREATE TEMP TABLE tmp_view_sizes AS
SELECT view_name, row_count,
    CASE 
        WHEN row_count < $1 THEN $2
        WHEN row_count < $3 THEN $4
        WHEN row_count < $5 THEN $6
        WHEN row_count < $7 THEN $8
        WHEN row_count < $9 THEN $10
        ELSE $11
    END AS size_category
FROM (
    -- For materialized views, use pg_class.reltuples (fast, uses cached stats)
    SELECT c.relname AS view_name, c.reltuples::bigint AS row_count
    FROM pg_class c
    JOIN pg_namespace n ON n.oid = c.relnamespace
    WHERE n.nspname = $12 AND c.relkind = $13
    UNION ALL
    -- For regular views, use reltuples from a LIMIT 0 query estimate or just 0
    -- Regular views don't have stats, so we just report them without counting
    SELECT viewname AS view_name, $14::bigint AS row_count
    FROM pg_views WHERE schemaname = $15
) v
ORDER BY row_count DESC

WITH view_counts AS (
    SELECT schemaname,
           viewname,
           row_count
    FROM cia_view_row_counts
    WHERE viewname != $1
      AND viewname != $2
      AND viewname NOT LIKE $3
      -- Skip views that timed out or had errors during Phase 1
      -- row_count: -1 = timeout, -2 = error, 0+ = success
      AND row_count >= $4
),
-- ============================================================================
-- TEMPORAL VIEW CLASSIFICATION
-- ============================================================================
-- Identify views by temporal granularity based on naming patterns
-- This enables stratified sampling across time periods
-- Uses reusable cia_classify_temporal_view() function
-- ============================================================================
view_temporal_classification AS (
    SELECT 
        vc.schemaname,
        vc.viewname,
        vc.row_count,
        c.temporal_granularity,
        c.samples_per_bucket
    FROM view_counts vc
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL cia_classify_temporal_view(vc.viewname) AS c
    -- CHANGED: Include ALL views, even empty ones (for header-only CSV files)
    -- WHERE vc.row_count > 0  -- REMOVED: No longer skip empty views
),
-- ============================================================================
-- TEMPORAL COLUMN DETECTION
-- ============================================================================
-- Automatically detect temporal columns in each view
-- ============================================================================
view_temporal_columns AS (
    SELECT 
        c.table_schema AS schemaname,
        c.table_name AS viewname,
        c.column_name AS temporal_column,
        c.data_type,
        -- Prioritize common temporal column names
        CASE 
            WHEN c.column_name IN ($5 /*, ... */) THEN $6
            WHEN c.column_name IN ($7 /*, ... */) THEN $8
            WHEN c.column_name IN ($9 /*, ... */) THEN $10
            WHEN c.column_name LIKE $11 THEN $12
            ELSE $13
        END AS priority
    FROM information_schema.columns c
    WHERE c.table_schema = $14
      AND c.data_type IN ($15 /*, ... */)
      AND c.column_name NOT IN ($16 /*, ... */) -- Exclude technical columns
),
view_primary_temporal_column AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT ON (schemaname, viewname)
        schemaname,
        viewname,
        temporal_column
    FROM view_temporal_columns
    ORDER BY schemaname, viewname, priority
),
-- ============================================================================
-- GENERATE STRATIFIED SAMPLING QUERIES
-- ============================================================================
view_extract AS (
    SELECT 
        vtc.schemaname,
        vtc.viewname,
        vtc.row_count,
        vtc.temporal_granularity,
        vtc.samples_per_bucket,
        vptc.temporal_column,
        -- Smart sample size calculation:
        -- 1. Complete extraction for small views (< threshold)
        -- 2. Extended samples for analytical trend views (election proximity, seasonal activity, career trajectory)
        -- 3. Extended samples for party/committee/person views
        -- 4. Extended samples for document/voting/worldbank views
        -- 5. Default sample size for others
        CASE 
            -- Complete extraction for small views
            WHEN vtc.row_count <= $17::int THEN vtc.row_count
            -- Extended samples for analytical trend views (500 rows for better temporal coverage)
            WHEN vtc.viewname IN ($18 /*, ... */) THEN
                LEAST($19::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for party-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $20 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $21 THEN 
                LEAST($22::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for committee-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $23 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $24 THEN 
                LEAST($25::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for person/politician views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $26 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $27 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $28 THEN 
                LEAST($29::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for document-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $30 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $31 THEN 
                LEAST($32::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for voting-related views
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $33 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $34 THEN 
                LEAST($35::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Extended samples for worldbank data
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $36 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $37 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $38 THEN 
                LEAST($39::int, vtc.row_count)
            -- Default sample size
            ELSE LEAST($40::int, vtc.row_count)
        END AS sample_rows,
        -- Extraction type for logging
        CASE 
            WHEN vtc.row_count <= $41::int THEN $42
            WHEN vtc.viewname IN ($43 /*, ... */) THEN $44
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $45 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $46 THEN $47
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $48 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $49 THEN $50
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $51 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $52 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $53 THEN $54
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $55 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $56 THEN $57
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $58 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $59 THEN $60
            WHEN vtc.viewname ILIKE $61 OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $62 
                OR vtc.viewname ILIKE $63 THEN $64
            ELSE $65
        END AS extraction_type,
        CASE WHEN vtc.viewname LIKE $66 THEN vtc.viewname ELSE $67 || vtc.viewname END AS file_prefix
    FROM view_temporal_classification vtc
    LEFT JOIN view_primary_temporal_column vptc 
        ON vtc.schemaname = vptc.schemaname 
        AND vtc.viewname = vptc.viewname
)
-- ============================================================================
-- GENERATE EXTRACTION COMMANDS WITH TEMPORAL STRATIFICATION
-- ============================================================================
SELECT 
    CASE 
        -- =====================================================================
        -- DAILY VIEWS: Stratified sampling by day
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $68 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $69 || $70 ||
                $71 ||
                $72 ||
                $73 ||
                $74 ||
                $75 ||
                $76 ||
                $77 ||
                $78 ||
                $79 || $80 ||
                $81 || $82 || $83 || $84 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $85 THEN $86 || $87
                    ELSE $88 || $89
                END,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname, temporal_column,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- WEEKLY VIEWS: Stratified sampling by week
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $90 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $91 || $92 ||
                $93 ||
                $94 ||
                $95 ||
                $96 ||
                $97 ||
                $98 ||
                $99 ||
                $100 ||
                $101 || $102 ||
                $103 || $104 || $105 || $106 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $107 THEN $108 || $109
                    ELSE $110 || $111
                END,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname, temporal_column,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- MONTHLY VIEWS: Stratified sampling by month
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $112 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $113 || $114 ||
                $115 ||
                $116 ||
                $117 ||
                $118 ||
                $119 ||
                $120 ||
                $121 ||
                $122 ||
                $123 || $124 ||
                $125 || $126 || $127 || $128 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $129 THEN $130 || $131
                    ELSE $132 || $133
                END,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname, temporal_column,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- ANNUAL VIEWS: Stratified sampling by year
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $134 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $135 || $136 ||
                $137 ||
                $138 ||
                $139 ||
                $140 ||
                $141 ||
                $142 ||
                $143 ||
                $144 || $145 ||
                $146 || $147 || $148 || $149 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $150 THEN $151 || $152
                    ELSE $153 || $154
                END,
                viewname,
                samples_per_bucket,
                temporal_column, schemaname, viewname,
                samples_per_bucket, sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- TEMPORAL TREND VIEWS: Sample across all time periods
        -- =====================================================================
        WHEN temporal_granularity = $155 AND temporal_column IS NOT NULL THEN
            format(
                $156 || $157 ||
                $158 ||
                $159 ||
                $160 ||
                $161 ||
                $162 ||
                $163 ||
                $164 ||
                $165 ||
                $166 || $167 ||
                $168 || $169 || $170 || $171 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $172 THEN $173 || $174
                    ELSE $175 || $176
                END,
                viewname,
                temporal_column, temporal_column, schemaname, viewname,
                sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix, file_prefix
            )
        
        -- =====================================================================
        -- NON-TEMPORAL VIEWS: Use random sampling with smart sample sizes
        -- =====================================================================
        ELSE
            format(
                $177 || $178 ||
                $179 || $180 ||
                $181 || $182 || $183 || $184 ||
                CASE 
                    WHEN row_count = $185 THEN $186 || $187
                    ELSE $188 || $189
                END,
                extraction_type,
                viewname,
                sample_rows,
                row_count,
                schemaname,
                viewname,
                sample_rows,
                file_prefix,
                viewname, row_count, row_count,
                file_prefix,
                file_prefix
            )
    END AS extraction_command
FROM view_extract
ORDER BY 
    CASE temporal_granularity
        WHEN $190 THEN $191
        WHEN $192 THEN $193
        WHEN $194 THEN $195
        WHEN $196 THEN $197
        WHEN $198 THEN $199
        ELSE $200
    END,
    viewname

CREATE TEMP TABLE tmp_empty_views AS
SELECT view_name, view_type, row_count,
    CASE 
        WHEN view_name LIKE $1 THEN $2
        WHEN view_name LIKE $3 THEN $4
        WHEN view_name LIKE $5 THEN $6
        WHEN view_name LIKE $7 THEN $8
        WHEN view_name LIKE $9 THEN $10
        WHEN view_name LIKE $11 THEN $12
        ELSE $13
    END AS view_category
FROM (
    -- For materialized views, use pg_class.reltuples (fast)
    SELECT c.relname AS view_name, $14 AS view_type, c.reltuples::bigint AS row_count
    FROM pg_class c
    JOIN pg_namespace n ON n.oid = c.relnamespace
    WHERE n.nspname = $15 AND c.relkind = $16
    -- Note: Regular views require actual count - skip them in empty check
    -- They were already counted in Phase 1
) v
WHERE row_count = $17
ORDER BY view_category, view_name
